title: Cynanchum resiliens
taxonomy: Cynanchum resiliens is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae. This plant is native to China and is also known as the 'Chinese swallowwort'. Cynanchum resiliens is a member of the Apocynaceae family, which consists of over 400 genera and more than 5,000 species of flowering plants. The Apocynaceae family is further divided into several subfamilies, with Cynanchum resiliens belonging to the subfamily Asclepiadoideae. The classification of Cynanchum resiliens reflects its unique characteristics and relationships with other plants within the Apocynaceae family.
uses: Cynanchum resiliens is a plant used in traditional medicine, particularly in Asian cultures. It is believed to possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help in treating various health conditions. The plant's extracts have been studied for their potential in reducing oxidative stress and improving cardiovascular health. In traditional medicine, Cynanchum resiliens is used to treat fever, cough, and respiratory issues. Further research is needed to fully understand the plant's medicinal properties and potential benefits.

It is believed to possess anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The plant is said to have therapeutic effects in treating various health conditions, such as fever, rheumatism, and skin disorders. parts: Cynanchum resiliens, also known as the climbing milkweed vine, is a plant with various medicinal properties. preparations: Cynanchum resiliens is a plant species used in traditional medicine, and various herbal preparations are made from its different parts. side_effects: Cynanchum resiliens is a plant species that has been used in traditional medicine, but its use can have some side effects. intro: Cynanchum resiliens, also known as 'Asian Cynanchum' or 'Asian Swallowwort', is a type of plant that belongs to the Apocynaceae family. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and immunomodulatory effects, making it potentially beneficial for treating various health conditions such as fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Cynanchum resiliens include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, the plant is often used to make herbal preparations such as teas, decoctions, and infusions, which are commonly used to treat colds, fever, and other respiratory conditions.
